findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 48. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The HTML fabricates an email address as a mailto link that is not explicitly present as a clickable link in the PDF. The PDF shows digital signature metadata with 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov, c=US' (with line break), but the HTML constructs a clean mailto link 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov'. While the email address content is derived from the PDF's digital signature block, wrapping it in an <a href='mailto:'> link is an invention.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov, c=US' (digital signature metadata). HTML: 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov'

findings.description: The HTML adds internal navigation anchor links in the table of contents that do not exist in the PDF. The PDF has a simple numbered outline list, but the HTML wraps each item in an <a href> linking to section IDs.
findings.evidence: PDF outline: 'I. History/Background' (plain text). HTML: 'History/Background'. These are accessibility enhancements, but noted for completeness.
